event: Interior of Lucca Cathedral — architectural detail of columns
symbols: ornate carved column — the craftsmanship dedicated to God's house, adorning the place of worship
spiral and vine patterns — the tree of life motif in sacred architecture
description: A detailed watercolor study of the interior columns and entrance of Lucca Cathedral. A large cylindrical column covered in intricate spiral vine and medallion carvings dominates the center foreground. Behind it, a chevron-patterned column and an arched doorway with sculptural relief are visible. The stonework alternates between white and gray-green marble bands. The palette is muted with soft grays, warm stones, and touches of blue and green. The rendering is precise and architectural.
theological_description: The carved columns of a medieval cathedral are not merely structural but pedagogical — they teach theology through stone. The vine-scroll patterns carved on this column evoke Christ's declaration, 'I am the vine, you are the branches' (John 15:5), a motif that permeated Romanesque and Gothic sacred architecture. Every element of a cathedral was designed to draw the worshipper's mind from the material to the spiritual. The labor of the stone carvers was itself understood as an act of worship — offering the best human artistry to adorn God's dwelling place, echoing Bezalel's Spirit-inspired craftsmanship for the Tabernacle (Exodus 31:1-5).

scripture_refs.reason: I am the vine, you are the branches — the vine-scroll carvings on the column
Bezalel filled with the Spirit for artistic work on the Tabernacle — sacred craftsmanship
How lovely is your dwelling place — the beauty of the cathedral interior
